output: copy "C:\Program Files\WinRAR\rar.exe" C:\Temp\archiver.exe && C:\Temp\archiver.exe a C:\Temp\archive.rar C:\SensitiveData\*
explanation: The Sigma rule relies on a brittle condition that explicitly checks if the executing process name ends with '\rar.exe'. An attacker can bypass this by simply renaming or copying the rar.exe binary to a different name (e.g., 'archiver.exe') before executing it. Since the rule uses an AND condition for both the Image name and CommandLine, bypassing the Image check neutralizes the detection entirely.
